commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Tokenjuice middleware normalization for bash/exec tool results whose content and command args are present but whose details are absent, plus a regression test for that path.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +42. Total +90 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows tokenjuice@0.8.0 requires completed/failed details, while current bash results can carry truncation metadata without that status, and the PR returns those metadata objects unchanged.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical repair: normalize exec-like details based on the tokenjuice status contract instead of the mere presence of an object.

target.title: Fix tokenjuice bash results without details
target.body:
## Summary
- compact bash/exec tool results even when the tool result has content and command args but no details object
- synthesize minimal completed/failed details so the existing Tokenjuice reducer can run
- add regression coverage for bash results without details

## Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/tokenjuice/index.test.ts
- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/tokenjuice/index.test.ts
- git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD

## Real behavior proof
After applying the installed @openclaw/tokenjuice middleware patch on a real local OpenClaw setup, I ran a bash tool-result middleware smoke with no details object and a real command/content-shaped event. Output:

```json
{
  "changed": true,
  "hasTokenjuice": true,
  "status": "completed",
  "rawChars": 24889
}
```

This proves the previously uncovered class now receives Tokenjuice metadata instead of passing through as un-compacted shell output.

## Baseline evidence
Local context-efficiency audit found only 5/967 recent exec-like results carrying Tokenjuice metadata before this fix, with noisy bash output dominating the compression candidate surface.
target.author: moeedahmed

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
